title: Race-lap grouped objective instead of plain binary loss
summary: Pit calls are relative within a race context: teams decide against same-lap competitors, tyre warm-up, and track-position windows.
rationale: Pit calls are relative within a race context: teams decide against same-lap competitors, tyre warm-up, and track-position windows. CatBoost’s QueryCrossEntropy explicitly models grouped binary outcomes with per-group shifts, which is a closer fit than independent rowwise logloss.
implementation_hint: Create groups such as Race_Year x LapNumber or Race_Year x estimated pit-window bucket, train a CatBoost model with QueryCrossEntropy or QuerySoftMax on top of the current features, and blend its OOF logits with the baseline classifier rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: May improve ranking among same-context candidates and add complementary signal to the current tree baseline, especially when several drivers are simultaneously near pit windows.
risk: If groups are too coarse or sparse, the grouped objective can become noisy and underperform plain logloss; careful grouping and OOF blending are required.
